channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Gene-edited lettuce turns green, boosting beneficial compounds
item.description: Researchers used genome editing to block red pigment production in lettuce, leading to an accumulation of other beneficial plant compounds.

item.title: Hidden fungal network beneath Earth spans 68 quadrillion miles
item.description: Scientists have mapped a vast underground fungal superhighway that supports much of life on Earth for the first time.

item.title: Oxford physicists create a stranger version of Schrödinger's cat
item.description: Oxford physicists have developed a new type of quantum state, similar to Schrödinger's cat, using highly quantum components.

item.title: Staple-shaped particles create material that can switch strength rapidly
item.description: Scientists have discovered that staple-shaped particles can tangle to form a material that is both strong and flexible, unlike conventional materials.
